{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Wealth Manager (Fintech or Asset Management Sector)",
  "description": "TopHire Africa is a timeless and innovative HR-Consulting Service provider that&#39;s geared towards delivering recruitment and training of qualitative verified talent.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Wealth Manager (Fintech or Asset Management Sector)&nbsp;

Job Location: Lagos&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities and Accountabilities
Investor Acquisition &amp; Retention:


	Pursue new investment opportunities by actively engaging potential investors, attending
	conferences, and networking within the industry to achieve deposit mobilization from approved sources.
	Collaborate with the Brands Marketing and Communications team to develop effective strategies to attract new investors and retain existing ones.
	Prepare investment proposals, pitch decks, and presentations tailored to the needs and preferences of specific investor segments.
	Conduct market research and analysis to identify investor preferences, market trends, and potential areas of growth.


Investor Relationship Management:


	Establish and maintain strong relationships with existing investors, including institutional investors, private equity firms, and high-net-worth individuals.
	Serve as the primary point of contact for investors, addressing their inquiries, providing regular updates and fostering open communication channels.
	Understand investor needs, expectations, and risk appetite, ensuring their investment objectives align with the institution&#39;s strategies.
	Develop a deep understanding of the institution&#39;s products, services, and investmentopportunities to effectively engage with investors.


Investor Communication:


	Develop and execute an effective investor relations communication strategy, including regular newsletters, investor presentations, and annual reports.
	Coordinate and lead investor meetings, conference calls, and presentations to convey the institution&#39;s financial performance, investment strategies, and growth prospects.


Salary
N1,000,000 - N1,500,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: tophireafrica@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-06-17",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "TopHire Africa"
    
  },

      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }

Wealth Manager (Fintech or Asset Management Sector) Job at TopHire Africa




        
    

        
        TopHire Africa is a timeless and innovative HR-Consulting Service provider that's geared towards delivering recruitment and training of qualitative verified talent.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Wealth Manager (Fintech or Asset Management Sector) 

Job Location: Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities and Accountabilities
Investor Acquisition & Retention:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Pursue new investment opportunities by actively engaging potential investors, attending
	conferences, and networking within the industry to achieve deposit mobilization from approved sources.
	Collaborate with the Brands Marketing and Communications team to develop effective strategies to attract new investors and retain existing ones.
	Prepare investment proposals, pitch decks, and presentations tailored to the needs and preferences of specific investor segments.
	Conduct market research and analysis to identify investor preferences, market trends, and potential areas of growth.


Investor Relationship Management:


	Establish and maintain strong relationships with existing investors, including institutional investors, private equity firms, and high-net-worth individuals.
	Serve as the primary point of contact for investors, addressing their inquiries, providing regular updates and fostering open communication channels.
	Understand investor needs, expectations, and risk appetite, ensuring their investment objectives align with the institution's strategies.
	Develop a deep understanding of the institution's products, services, and investmentopportunities to effectively engage with investors.


Investor Communication:


	Develop and execute an effective investor relations communication strategy, including regular newsletters, investor presentations, and annual reports.
	Coordinate and lead investor meetings, conference calls, and presentations to convey the institution's financial performance, investment strategies, and growth prospects.


Salary
N1,000,000 - N1,500,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE) Officer",
  "description": "Tophire Africa - Our client, One of the largest downstream, midstream &amp; upstream in Nigeria, is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE) Officer

Job Location: Imo
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Oil &amp; Gas (One of the largest downstream, midstream &amp; upstream in Nigeria)

About the Role


	We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented Health, Safety and Environment (HSE) Officer to support field operations at the Flow Station.
	The successful candidate will ensure full compliance with company HSE standards, regulatory requirements, and industry best practices while promoting a strong safety culture across all operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Ensure compliance with all company HSE policies, procedures, and regulatory requirements
	Communicate HSE expectations to employees and contractors on site
	Conduct daily site inspections, tours, and safety observations
	Prepare and submit daily, weekly, and monthly HSE reports
	Review contractor HSE plans, JHAs, PTWs, and related documentation
	Conduct pre-mob inspections of contractor equipment and personnel certifications
	Support emergency preparedness and coordinate drills and evacuation procedures
	Monitor fire and gas detection systems and safety-critical equipment
	Ensure compliance with Management of Change (MOC) procedures
	Maintain oil spill response readiness and coordinate spill response activities
	Participate in regulatory inspections and audits (DPR, NOSDRA, etc.)
	Coordinate incident reporting, investigations, and corrective action tracking
	Oversee Permit to Work (PTW) systems and ensure proper close-out
	Monitor lifting operations and other safety-critical activities
	Maintain HSE registers, incident records, and risk assessments
	Facilitate HSE committee meetings and toolbox talks
	Support operational safety studies and continuous improvement initiatives


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	HSE regulatory compliance performance
	Lost Time Injury (LTI/LTA) and incident rates
	Accuracy and timeliness of HSE reporting
	Quality of inspections and investigations
	Effectiveness of risk management processes


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree or HND in Sciences, Engineering, or related field with a minimum of 5 years of experience in an HSE role in the Oil &amp; Gas industry
	NEBOSH International General Certificate (added advantage)
	Knowledge of oil &amp; gas safety regulations and operational standards
	Familiarity with ISO, OSHA, ISM, and ISPS standards.


Remuneration
N800,000 - N1,000,000 / Month (Above industry standard)

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: tophireafrica@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: Candidates willing to relocate can apply.",

  },

Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE) Officer Job at Tophire Africa




        
    

        
        Tophire Africa - Our client, One of the largest downstream, midstream & upstream in Nigeria, is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE) Officer

Job Location: Imo
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Oil & Gas (One of the largest downstream, midstream & upstream in Nigeria)

About the Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented Health, Safety and Environment (HSE) Officer to support field operations at the Flow Station.
	The successful candidate will ensure full compliance with company HSE standards, regulatory requirements, and industry best practices while promoting a strong safety culture across all operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Ensure compliance with all company HSE policies, procedures, and regulatory requirements
	Communicate HSE expectations to employees and contractors on site
	Conduct daily site inspections, tours, and safety observations
	Prepare and submit daily, weekly, and monthly HSE reports
	Review contractor HSE plans, JHAs, PTWs, and related documentation
	Conduct pre-mob inspections of contractor equipment and personnel certifications
	Support emergency preparedness and coordinate drills and evacuation procedures
	Monitor fire and gas detection systems and safety-critical equipment
	Ensure compliance with Management of Change (MOC) procedures
	Maintain oil spill response readiness and coordinate spill response activities
	Participate in regulatory inspections and audits (DPR, NOSDRA, etc.)
	Coordinate incident reporting, investigations, and corrective action tracking
	Oversee Permit to Work (PTW) systems and ensure proper close-out
	Monitor lifting operations and other safety-critical activities
	Maintain HSE registers, incident records, and risk assessments
	Facilitate HSE committee meetings and toolbox talks
	Support operational safety studies and continuous improvement initiatives


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	HSE regulatory compliance performance
	Lost Time Injury (LTI/LTA) and incident rates
	Accuracy and timeliness of HSE reporting
	Quality of inspections and investigations
	Effectiveness of risk management processes


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree or HND in Sciences, Engineering, or related field with a minimum of 5 years of experience in an HSE role in the Oil & Gas industry
	NEBOSH International General Certificate (added advantage)
	Knowledge of oil & gas safety regulations and operational standards
	Familiarity with ISO, OSHA, ISM, and ISPS standards.


Remuneration
N800,000 - N1,000,000 / Month (Above industry standard)

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: Candidates willing to relocate can apply.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

  "description": "Odixcity Consulting is an International Hr consulting and procurement firm. With expertise in Recruitment &amp; Selection, Performance management, Training &amp; Development, Compensation and benefits as well as Outsourcing of goods and services for organizations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Digital Marketer / Graphics Designer
Job Location: Port Harcourt - Rivers

Job Summary


	A Digital Marketer develops, implements, and manages online marketing campaigns across platforms such as social media, search engines, email, websites, and paid advertising channels.
	They analyze campaign performance, optimize strategies using data insights, and work to improve brand visibility and customer acquisition.


Responsibilities


	Plan and execute full-funnel campaigns (awareness - consideration - conversion).
	Write compelling ad copy, social captions, email subject lines, and landing page headlines.
	Use Google Analytics 4, Meta Ads Manager, and Google Tag Manager to track conversions, build dashboards, and present weekly performance inisights.
	Contionusly test ad formats, visuals, audeiences, and CTAs to improve results.
	Stay ahead of social media algorithm changes, ad policy updates, and design trends.
	Plan, schedule, and publish daily content across 3-5 platforms.
	Monitor social listening for brand mentions, industry trends, and customer pain points.
	Track and report on engagement rate, reach, follower growth, and sentiment analysis.
	Design landing page visuals, email headers, and presentation decks.


Requirements


	3+ years of experience working as a digital marketer and graphic designer.
	Proven experience setting up, launcing, and optimizing paid ad campaigns on Meta and Google Ads.
	Comfortable with audience targeting, retargeting, lookalike audeiences, and bid strategies.
	Ability to create and execute a content calendar aligned with product launches, promotions, and seasonal trends.
	Ability to design high-converting ad creatives, carousels, stories, reels covers, infographics, and simple banners.
	Basic knowledge of SEO (On-page and keyword research).
	Experience with email marketing tools (Mailchimp, Brevo etc).
	Familiarity with Shopify, Wordpress, or Webflow.
	Must be willling to come for physical interview.


Renumeration
NGN200,000 per month.&nbsp;

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV&rsquo;s and link to their portfolio to: cvs@odixcityconsulting.com.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

        Odixcity Consulting is an International Hr consulting and procurement firm. With expertise in Recruitment & Selection, Performance management, Training & Development, Compensation and benefits as well as Outsourcing of goods and services for organizations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Digital Marketer / Graphics Designer
Job Location: Port Harcourt - Rivers

Job Summary

	A Digital Marketer develops, implements, and manages online marketing campaigns across platforms such as social media, search engines, email, websites, and paid advertising channels.
	They analyze campaign performance, optimize strategies using data insights, and work to improve brand visibility and customer acquisition.


Responsibilities


	Plan and execute full-funnel campaigns (awareness - consideration - conversion).
	Write compelling ad copy, social captions, email subject lines, and landing page headlines.

	Stay ahead of social media algorithm changes, ad policy updates, and design trends.
	Plan, schedule, and publish daily content across 3-5 platforms.
	Monitor social listening for brand mentions, industry trends, and customer pain points.
	Track and report on engagement rate, reach, follower growth, and sentiment analysis.
	Design landing page visuals, email headers, and presentation decks.


Requirements


	3+ years of experience working as a digital marketer and graphic designer.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "ERP Sales Executive",
  "description": "Welcome to Endeavour Solution Nigeria Limited, your premier IT partner rooted in the heart of Africa. We specialize in delivering innovative and robust technology solutions designed to meet the ever-evolving needs of modern businesses.

In today&rsquo;s fast-paced IT landscape, stability and reliable support aren&rsquo;t just optional&mdash;they&rsquo;re essential. That&rsquo;s why we offer round-the-clock support and seamless technology integration to ensure your systems are always at their best. At Endeavour Solution Nigeria Limited, we don&rsquo;t just provide services; we create comprehensive solutions that fit perfectly within your organization. Discover how we can help you stay ahead in the digital age.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: ERP Sales Executive

Job Location: Ilupeju, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	A leading Tech Solutions firm is seeking to hire an experienced and results-driven ERP Sales Executive to drive business growth and expand its client base.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for identifying prospects, closing ERP software sales, managing client relationships, and developing new business opportunities across various industries.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify, generate, and convert new business opportunities for ERP solutions.
	Actively sell ERP software and related technology solutions to prospective and existing clients.
	Conduct client needs assessments and recommend suitable ERP solutions.
	Build and maintain strong client relationships to ensure customer satisfaction and repeat business.
	Conduct outreach through calls, emails, networking, and meetings to engage potential customers and schedule product demonstrations.
	Develop and manage a strong sales pipeline using online and offline channels.
	Achieve assigned sales targets and contribute to overall revenue growth.
	Prepare and deliver compelling sales presentations, proposals, and product demonstrations.
	Monitor market trends, competitor activities, and industry developments to identify new sales opportunities.
	Collaborate with technical and implementation teams to ensure seamless onboarding and service delivery.
	Prepare sales reports by collecting, analyzing, and summarizing sales data.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Marketing, Business Administration, Computer Science, or a related field.
	2&ndash;4 years of proven experience in ERP software sales or enterprise technology solutions sales.
	Strong understanding of ERP systems and business process solutions.
	Proven ability to meet and exceed sales targets.
	Strong sales, negotiation, and relationship management skills.
	Excellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills.
	Self-motivated, target-driven, and results-oriented.
	Ability to understand client business needs and provide tailored solutions.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools and CRM systems is an added advantage.
	Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team environment.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs to: jobapplicationexpress@gmail.com using &ldquo;ERP Sales Executive &ndash; ESNL&rdquo; as the subject of the email.",

ERP Sales Executive Job at Endeavour Solution Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Welcome to Endeavour Solution Nigeria Limited, your premier IT partner rooted in the heart of Africa. We specialize in delivering innovative and robust technology solutions designed to meet the ever-evolving needs of modern businesses.

In today’s fast-paced IT landscape, stability and reliable support aren’t just optional—they’re essential. That’s why we offer round-the-clock support and seamless technology integration to ensure your systems are always at their best. At Endeavour Solution Nigeria Limited, we don’t just provide services; we create comprehensive solutions that fit perfectly within your organization. Discover how we can help you stay ahead in the digital age.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Position: ERP Sales Executive

Job Location: Ilupeju, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	A leading Tech Solutions firm is seeking to hire an experienced and results-driven ERP Sales Executive to drive business growth and expand its client base.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for identifying prospects, closing ERP software sales, managing client relationships, and developing new business opportunities across various industries.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify, generate, and convert new business opportunities for ERP solutions.
	Actively sell ERP software and related technology solutions to prospective and existing clients.
	Conduct client needs assessments and recommend suitable ERP solutions.
	Build and maintain strong client relationships to ensure customer satisfaction and repeat business.
	Conduct outreach through calls, emails, networking, and meetings to engage potential customers and schedule product demonstrations.
	Develop and manage a strong sales pipeline using online and offline channels.
	Achieve assigned sales targets and contribute to overall revenue growth.
	Prepare and deliver compelling sales presentations, proposals, and product demonstrations.
	Monitor market trends, competitor activities, and industry developments to identify new sales opportunities.
	Collaborate with technical and implementation teams to ensure seamless onboarding and service delivery.
	Prepare sales reports by collecting, analyzing, and summarizing sales data.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Marketing, Business Administration, Computer Science, or a related field.
	2–4 years of proven experience in ERP software sales or enterprise technology solutions sales.
	Strong understanding of ERP systems and business process solutions.
	Proven ability to meet and exceed sales targets.
	Strong sales, negotiation, and relationship management skills.
	Excellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills.
	Self-motivated, target-driven, and results-oriented.
	Ability to understand client business needs and provide tailored solutions.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools and CRM systems is an added advantage.
	Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team environment.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs to: [email protected] using “ERP Sales Executive – ESNL” as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
